extraction.one_line_summary: VAN DER POEL Mathieu bridged from chasing group in final 500m on steep Loreto Wall finish to overtake solo leader FABRO who attacked 30km out.
tactical_patterns: FABRO Matteo solo attack from 30km+ suited accumulated fatigue conditions where chasers couldn't organize smooth pursuit.
VAN DER POEL waited until steepest 500m section before launching decisive bridge, using puncheur power when gradient maximized.
GUERRERO Ruben bridged first but couldn't hold VAN DER POEL's final acceleration, revealing pure classics power differential.
GC contenders YATES/THOMAS/VLASOV rode together up Loreto Wall, effectively neutralizing time gaps before TT decider.

rider_signals.FABRO Matteo: positive: held 30km+ solo break to within 100m of stage win on maximum gradient finish despite accumulated week fatigue
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Mathieu: positive: demonstrated precise tactical patience by waiting for steepest 500m gradient section before committing to bridge effort
rider_signals.GUERRERO Ruben: neutral: bridged strongest initially but lacked explosive power to counter VAN DER POEL's final acceleration on maximum gradient
rider_signals.KELDERMAN Wilco: positive: finished 4th despite not being breakaway rider, suggesting stronger climbing form than expected on steep finishes
rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: admitted post-race he wants larger GC advantage with only 16sec over MICA before TT, revealing confidence concerns
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: neutral: rode conservatively with GC group at 39sec deficit, explicitly banking on TT superiority rather than attacking
